title: MarginalizationsCommute
lean_code_with_comments:
theorem marginalize_comm (G : CDMG Node) (W₁ W₂ : Finset Node)
    (hW₁ : W₁ ⊆ G.V) (hW₂ : W₂ ⊆ G.V) (hDisj : Disjoint W₁ W₂) :
    (G.marginalize W₁ hW₁).marginalize W₂
        (subset_sdiff_of_disjoint hW₂ hDisj.symm)
      = G.marginalize (W₁ ∪ W₂) (Finset.union_subset hW₁ hW₂)
    ∧
    (G.marginalize W₂ hW₂).marginalize W₁
        (subset_sdiff_of_disjoint hW₁ hDisj)
      = G.marginalize (W₁ ∪ W₂) (Finset.union_subset hW₁ hW₂)
-- claim_3_17 -- end statement
:= by
  -- ## CDMG extensionality.
  have cdmgExt : ∀ {G₁ G₂ : CDMG Node},
      G₁.J = G₂.J → G₁.V = G₂.V → G₁.E = G₂.E → G₁.L = G₂.L → G₁ = G₂ := by
    rintro ⟨J₁, V₁, _, E₁, _, L₁, _, _, _⟩
           ⟨J₂, V₂, _, E₂, _, L₂, _, _, _⟩ hJ hV hE hL
    obtain rfl := hJ; obtain rfl := hV; obtain rfl := hE; obtain rfl := hL; rfl
  -- ## L-field equality.
  have hL_field_eq : ∀ (S T : Finset Node) (hS : S ⊆ G.V) (hT : T ⊆ G.V)
      (hDisj_ST : Disjoint S T),
      ((G.marginalize S hS).marginalize T
          (subset_sdiff_of_disjoint hT hDisj_ST.symm)).L
        = (G.marginalize (S ∪ T) (Finset.union_subset hS hT)).L := by
    intro S T hS hT hDisj_ST
    exact marg_L_field_eq S T hS hT hDisj_ST
  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
  · refine cdmgExt rfl ?_ ?_ ?_
    · exact sdiff_sdiff_left
    · exact marg_E_field_eq W₁ W₂ hW₁ hW₂ hDisj
    · exact hL_field_eq W₁ W₂ hW₁ hW₂ hDisj
  · -- (b): apply the auxiliaries with the roles of W₁/W₂ swapped.
    have heq : G.marginalize (W₂ ∪ W₁) (Finset.union_subset hW₂ hW₁)
             = G.marginalize (W₁ ∪ W₂) (Finset.union_subset hW₁ hW₂) := by
      congr 1
      exact Finset.union_comm W₂ W₁
    refine cdmgExt rfl ?_ ?_ ?_
    · change (G.V \ W₂) \ W₁ = G.V \ (W₁ ∪ W₂)
      rw [Finset.union_comm W₁ W₂]
      exact sdiff_sdiff_left
    · have h := marg_E_field_eq W₂ W₁ hW₂ hW₁ hDisj.symm
      rw [heq] at h
      exact h
    · have h := hL_field_eq W₂ W₁ hW₂ hW₁ hDisj.symm
      rw [heq] at h
      exact h
lean_code_without_comments:
theorem marginalize_comm (G : CDMG Node) (W₁ W₂ : Finset Node)
    (hW₁ : W₁ ⊆ G.V) (hW₂ : W₂ ⊆ G.V) (hDisj : Disjoint W₁ W₂) :
    (G.marginalize W₁ hW₁).marginalize W₂
        (subset_sdiff_of_disjoint hW₂ hDisj.symm)
      = G.marginalize (W₁ ∪ W₂) (Finset.union_subset hW₁ hW₂)
    ∧
    (G.marginalize W₂ hW₂).marginalize W₁
        (subset_sdiff_of_disjoint hW₁ hDisj)
      = G.marginalize (W₁ ∪ W₂) (Finset.union_subset hW₁ hW₂)

:= by

  have cdmgExt : ∀ {G₁ G₂ : CDMG Node},
      G₁.J = G₂.J → G₁.V = G₂.V → G₁.E = G₂.E → G₁.L = G₂.L → G₁ = G₂ := by
    rintro ⟨J₁, V₁, _, E₁, _, L₁, _, _, _⟩
           ⟨J₂, V₂, _, E₂, _, L₂, _, _, _⟩ hJ hV hE hL
    obtain rfl := hJ; obtain rfl := hV; obtain rfl := hE; obtain rfl := hL; rfl

  have hL_field_eq : ∀ (S T : Finset Node) (hS : S ⊆ G.V) (hT : T ⊆ G.V)
      (hDisj_ST : Disjoint S T),
      ((G.marginalize S hS).marginalize T
          (subset_sdiff_of_disjoint hT hDisj_ST.symm)).L
        = (G.marginalize (S ∪ T) (Finset.union_subset hS hT)).L := by
    intro S T hS hT hDisj_ST
    exact marg_L_field_eq S T hS hT hDisj_ST
  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
  · refine cdmgExt rfl ?_ ?_ ?_
    · exact sdiff_sdiff_left
    · exact marg_E_field_eq W₁ W₂ hW₁ hW₂ hDisj
    · exact hL_field_eq W₁ W₂ hW₁ hW₂ hDisj
  ·
    have heq : G.marginalize (W₂ ∪ W₁) (Finset.union_subset hW₂ hW₁)
             = G.marginalize (W₁ ∪ W₂) (Finset.union_subset hW₁ hW₂) := by
      congr 1
      exact Finset.union_comm W₂ W₁
    refine cdmgExt rfl ?_ ?_ ?_
    · change (G.V \ W₂) \ W₁ = G.V \ (W₁ ∪ W₂)
      rw [Finset.union_comm W₁ W₂]
      exact sdiff_sdiff_left
    · have h := marg_E_field_eq W₂ W₁ hW₂ hW₁ hDisj.symm
      rw [heq] at h
      exact h
    · have h := hL_field_eq W₂ W₁ hW₂ hW₁ hDisj.symm
      rw [heq] at h
      exact h
lean_explanation:
The theorem `marginalize_comm` quantifies over a CDMG `G : CDMG Node` over the ambient vertex type `Node : Type*` (with the `[DecidableEq Node]` instance inherited from the file-level `variable` block, which `Finset`-backed membership and filter operations consume). The two marginalisation sets appear as explicit `Finset Node` arguments `W₁` and `W₂`, matching the LN's $W_1$ and $W_2$. The LN's side conditions $W_1 \subseteq V$ and $W_2 \subseteq V$ are recorded as explicit hypotheses `hW₁ : W₁ ⊆ G.V` and `hW₂ : W₂ ⊆ G.V`, and the disjointness $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$ is rendered as `hDisj : Disjoint W₁ W₂` using Mathlib's lattice-theoretic `Disjoint` predicate (which on `Finset` unfolds to the literal $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$). The conclusion is a conjunction `(a) ∧ (b)` corresponding to the (a)/(b) decomposition of the LN's triple equality.

Mathlib idioms worth flagging: `Finset.union_subset hW₁ hW₂` packages the fact that $W_1, W_2 \subseteq V \Rightarrow W_1 \cup W_2 \subseteq V$, serving as the well-typedness witness for the joint marginalisation `G.marginalize (W₁ ∪ W₂) (…)` on the right-hand side. Each nested marginalisation on the left, e.g. `(G.marginalize W₁ hW₁).marginalize W₂ …`, needs a proof that `W₂` is a subset of the inner-marginalised carrier `(G.marginalize W₁ hW₁).V = G.V \ W₁`; this is supplied by the file-local helper `subset_sdiff_of_disjoint`, a one-liner around `Finset.subset_sdiff.mpr` that consumes the carrier-subset hypothesis (`W₂ ⊆ G.V`) together with the disjointness witness (`Disjoint W₂ W₁`) and lands at the required sdiff form `W₂ ⊆ G.V \ W₁`. The `hDisj.symm` flip at one call site and direct use at the other reflect the two orientations the helper is consumed in for the (a) and (b) branches.

The shape of the proof body: it opens with an inline `cdmgExt` extensionality lemma capturing CDMG-structure injectivity (two `CDMG Node` values are equal once their four data fields `J`, `V`, `E`, `L` agree, because the five propositional fields are discharged by proof irrelevance). Each of (a) and (b) is then reduced via `cdmgExt` to four field-by-field equalities: the `J` field is `rfl` (marginalisation fixes the input set), the `V` field is `sdiff_sdiff_left` (the standard $(A \setminus B) \setminus C = A \setminus (B \cup C)$ identity), and the `E` and `L` fields are delegated to two parametric file-local auxiliaries `marg_E_field_eq` and `marg_L_field_eq`. The (b) branch additionally rewrites `W₂ ∪ W₁` to `W₁ ∪ W₂` via `Finset.union_comm`, which is the Lean rendering of the tex's "by symmetry" closing step.
design_choices:
**One theorem returning a conjunction, not two separate top-level theorems.** The LN packages its triple equality $(G^{\setminus W_1})^{\setminus W_2} = (G^{\setminus W_2})^{\setminus W_1} = G^{\setminus (W_1 \cup W_2)}$ inside a single lemma block, and the canonical statement decomposes that triple into the conjunction of two binary equalities (a) $(G^{\setminus W_1})^{\setminus W_2} = G^{\setminus (W_1 \cup W_2)}$ and (b) $(G^{\setminus W_2})^{\setminus W_1} = G^{\setminus (W_1 \cup W_2)}$. We mirror that single-lemma packaging by returning `(a) ∧ (b)` from one theorem. Consumers reach `.1` for (a) and `.2` for (b); the LN's swap-symmetry reading $(G^{\setminus W_1})^{\setminus W_2} = (G^{\setminus W_2})^{\setminus W_1}$ is then recovered as `.1.trans .2.symm`, with no need to bake a third sub-claim into the statement. Conjunction order (a) ∧ (b) matches the LN reading order, so the natural `.1` / `.2` projections line up with the (a) / (b) labels. Splitting into two top-level theorems was rejected because it would duplicate the antecedents `hW₁`, `hW₂`, `hDisj` at each theorem head and diverge from the single-block packaging.

**Three independent hypotheses, not derived subset proofs baked into the binders.** The LN's premise block lists three independent facts: $W_1 \subseteq V$, $W_2 \subseteq V$, and $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$. We mirror that exactly with `hW₁ : W₁ ⊆ G.V`, `hW₂ : W₂ ⊆ G.V`, and `hDisj : Disjoint W₁ W₂`. The alternative — baking the derived fact `W₂ ⊆ G.V \ W₁` (the form the inner marginalisation actually needs) into a binder so that branch type-checks for free — was rejected because it would conflate the LN's clean premise list with internal calculation about `marginalize`'s domain, would force every call site to discharge the less-natural sdiff-subset on a plate, and would break the LN-level symmetry between the (a) and (b) readings (one binder would carry `W₂ ⊆ G.V \ W₁`, the other `W₁ ⊆ G.V \ W₂`, doubling the plumbing). The two derived subset proofs are instead supplied at the marginalisation call sites *inside the signature* via the helper `subset_sdiff_of_disjoint`, keeping the binder list isomorphic to the LN's premise list.

**`Disjoint W₁ W₂` (typeclass form), not `W₁ ∩ W₂ = ∅` (literal set form).** The two are equivalent on `Finset Node` (Mathlib's `Finset.disjoint_iff_inter_eq_empty`). We pick the `Disjoint`-typeclass form because Mathlib's `Finset.subset_sdiff` lemma is phrased natively against `Disjoint`, so the helper `subset_sdiff_of_disjoint` consumes it without a wrapper rewrite, and because `Disjoint` is the canonical shape used throughout the chapter (the underlying CDMG definition's $J / V$ disjointness, the marginalisation operator's preserved-disjointness field, and sibling intervention-commutation lemmas). Semantic content is identical to the LN's literal $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$.

**CDMG equality is read field-wise, but the unpacking is deferred to the proof.** Equality of two `CDMG`s unfolds via structure injectivity to the conjunction of equalities on the four data fields `J`, `V`, `E`, `L` (the propositional fields are discharged by proof irrelevance). We do not bake the field-wise unpacking into the *statement*; the proof opens with a one-line inline `cdmgExt` and reduces each of (a) and (b) to its four field-by-field checks. The vacuously-disjoint degenerate cases ($W_1 = W_2 = \emptyset$, $W_1 = \emptyset$, $W_2 = \emptyset$) are admitted by the signature, with the triple equality collapsing accordingly (e.g. $W_1 = W_2 = \emptyset$ reduces to $G = G = G$); the signature does not pre-emptively exclude them.
